facts.statement: Palantir Technologies Inc. is listed in SEC submissions metadata under the SIC description "Services-Prepackaged Software," trades on Nasdaq, and uses ticker PLTR.
Palantir's Class A common stock, par value $0.001 per share, is registered on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC under trading symbol PLTR.
Palantir reports two operating segments: Government and Commercial.
For the year ended December 31, 2025, Palantir reported total revenue of $4.475 billion.
In 2025, Government revenue was $2.402 billion, expenses attributable to the government segment were $826.2 million, and Government contribution was $1.576 billion, or 66% of Government revenue.
In 2025, Commercial revenue was $2.073 billion, expenses attributable to the commercial segment were $706.5 million, and Commercial contribution was $1.367 billion, or 66% of Commercial revenue.
Total contribution was $2.943 billion in 2025, equal to 66% of total revenue, up from 60% in 2024 and 56% in 2023 as presented in the segment table.
Palantir reported income from operations of $1.414 billion for 2025, compared with $310.4 million in 2024 and $120.0 million in 2023.
Net income attributable to common stockholders was $1.625 billion in 2025; basic EPS was $0.69 and diluted EPS was $0.63.

In its contribution-to-operating-income reconciliation for 2025, Palantir listed research and development expenses excluding stock-based compensation of $420.8 million and total stock-based compensation expense of $684.0 million.
In 2025, U.S. revenue was $3.320 billion, or 74% of total revenue; U.K. revenue was $427.4 million, or 10%; and rest-of-world revenue was $728.0 million, or 16%.

Palantir states that revenue by geography is based on the customer's headquarters or agency location at the time of sale, and that no country other than the U.S. or U.K. represented 10% or more of total revenue in 2025, 2024, or 2023.
The SEC company facts summary lists cash and equivalents of $831.0 million and stockholders' equity of $5.003 billion for FY2025.

As of December 31, 2025, Palantir maintained a full valuation allowance against potential future benefits for U.S. federal, state, and certain foreign deferred tax assets; the valuation allowance totaled $3.5 billion at year-end 2025.
As of February 10, 2026, Palantir had 2.291 billion Class A shares, 99.200 million Class B shares, and 1.005 million Class F shares outstanding.

For the 2026 annual meeting record date, each Class A share had one vote, each Class B share had ten votes and was convertible into one Class A share, and Class F shares had the votes described in the proxy's voting structure disclosure.

The proxy states that shares subject to the Founder Voting Agreement included 20.166 million Class A shares and 91.130 million Class B shares, and that the Founder Voting Trust held 1.005 million Class F shares with sole voting power.

The 2025 Form 10-K was signed by Alexander C. Karp as Chief Executive Officer and Director, Stephen Cohen as President and Director, David Glazer as Chief Financial Officer, and Jeffrey Buckley as Chief Accounting Officer.
The 2026 proxy asks stockholders to elect seven directors to serve until the next annual meeting and until their respective successors are elected and qualified.

Management, with participation and supervision of the CEO and CFO, concluded that Palantir's disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of December 31, 2025, and management concluded internal control over financial reporting was effective as of that date.

Alexander Karp beneficially owns non-commercial aircraft used for business and personal travel under Palantir's security program; Palantir incurred $17.2 million of expenses related to the executive aircraft in 2025.

In 2025, Palantir received $17.66 million for products and services from Anduril and $167,782 from Chapter, companies in which Peter Thiel may be deemed to have more than 10% beneficial ownership through related entities; Palantir also invested $40 million in Anduril's Series G preferred stock, representing less than 1% ownership of Anduril's outstanding preferred stock and aggregate outstanding stock as of December 31, 2025.

Palantir's related-person transaction policy gives the Audit Committee primary responsibility for reviewing, approving, ratifying, or disapproving transactions over $120,000 involving related persons, considering factors including director independence and whether terms are no less favorable than those generally available to an unaffiliated third party.

Palantir's 2025 Form 10-K identifies risks and uncertainties including demand for its platforms, ability to add customers and revenue, ability to integrate platforms for customers, ability to develop and timely launch new platforms or enhancements, competition, AI-related laws and regulations, and maintaining platform security and availability.
